title: woodsia ilvensis var. glabella
intro: Woodsia ilvensis var. glabella, also known as the glabella woodsia fern, is a species of fern belonging to the genus Woodsia and is characterized by its delicate fronds and unique leaf arrangement. Traditionally, the glabella woodsia fern has been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection. The health benefits of glabella woodsia fern include its ability to support immune function, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the glabella woodsia fern is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and may be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
